title: Xenial Moroccan Lamb Kefta Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish with a funky twist, featuring lamb kefta and a blend of aromatic spices, cooked in a Dutch oven with a yuzu cure and ayurvedic tamasic friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb, bread flour, yuzu juice, olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Remove the bowl from the refrigerator and use your hands to shape the mixture into small kefta, about 1 1/2 inches long and 1 inch in diameter.
Heat a couple of tablespoons of oil in the Dutch oven over medium-high heat, then add the kefta and cook, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add a couple of cups of water to the Dutch oven, cover it with a lid, and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the kefta, covered, for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving the kefta and sauce over couscous or rice.
